variant_protein: p.Arg93Cys (R93C); ancestral Arg93 -> derived Cys93; exon 2; EGF-like repeat near N-terminus
variant_cdna: not_reported (paper gives protein + genomic only; no c. notation)
rsid: not_reported / absent from dbSNP & 1000 Genomes at publication
genotype_or_zygosity: recessive; TT=93C/93C (blond); CT=heterozygous (dark); CC=93R/93R (dark). Resequencing (8 TYRP1 exons, 12 blond+12 dark): all 12 blond = TT; dark = CT (N=5) or CC (N=7)
phenotype: Blond hair (recessive); PolyPhen2 'probably damaging' post.prob 0.999; predicted to reduce catalytic activity
effect_or_association: Largest reported genetic effect on a visible human phenotype; ~46% of hair-color variance

gene: TYRP1 region / 9p23 (tag SNP; nearest-gene/tag assignment, not causal)
genomic_coord_build: 9p23 (GRCh37/hg19 context); exact coordinate not reported for this SNP
phenotype: Blond hair (case-control GWA, 43 blond vs 42 dark)

genotype_or_zygosity: R93C genotyped directly in discovery GWA panel
effect_or_association: P=9.60x10-23 (lowest of all 589,242 tested SNPs; stronger than top GWA SNP)

source_location: Table S4 (supplement docx); Main text p3

effect_or_association: Unbiased cohort (N=640, no phenotype selection): 93C freq=25.7% (~0.26), out of HWE chi2=32.92 P<0.0001 (excess homozygotes, deficit hets). Hair-selected biased cohort: 37.5%, HWE chi2=12.34 P=0.0021

genotype_or_zygosity: 3 x 93C-homozygous individuals had one parent from outside Solomon Is (2 Fiji, 1 New Guinea)
phenotype: Blond hair in Melanesia arises via TYRP1 R93C, a DIFFERENT genetic mechanism than European blond hair; 93C restricted to the Pacific
effect_or_association: Blond prevalence 5-10% in Oceania (highest outside Europe); European blond determinant alleles are absent here and 93C is absent in Europe

genotype_or_zygosity: TT (93C hom / blond) vs CT (het) vs CC (93R hom)
effect_or_association: TT blonds: NO significant darkening with age R2=0.013 P=0.187; CT het: darkening R2=0.026 P=0.0017; CC 93R hom: darkening R2=0.031 P=0.0004. 93R-hom vs het differ (one-sided T P=1.27x10-9 -> some co-dominant effect). Females lighter than males P=4.09x10-35
